mechanism_summary: Plain havoc BytesDeleteMutator shrinks input below 128-byte header minimum so Read returns a count != 1; ctx/I2S techniques keep inputs large and valid, anchoring to the well-formed path.
one_line: havoc shrinks input below 128-byte header; ctx/I2S anchor large valid seeds

mechanism_summary: naive havoc mutates IDAT bytes to leave residual compressed data after Z_STREAM_END; naive_ctx's context-indexed edge map floods from the deep inflate loop, starving byte mutation needed to create the extra-data condition.
one_line: naive havoc creates IDAT residual bytes; ctx floods edge map from deep inflate loop

mechanism_summary: naive's undiluted havoc builds long CR/LF-bearing char-data runs that keep the do-while loop alive; ctx coverage fragments the tight scan-loop edges across call contexts, diluting feedback and preventing assembly of those runs.
one_line: ctx dilutes scan-loop edge signal; naive assembles long CR/LF char-data run

mechanism_summary: naive's flat per-edge feedback immediately rewards the byte mutation landing name[1]='m'/'M'; ctx coverage splits the parser edges across call-context slots, diluting the signal that should guide mutation toward the xml-prefixed PI target.
one_line: ctx dilutes per-edge signal for name[1]=='m'/'M'; naive rewards it immediately

mechanism_summary: naive havoc places paired SHIN (0x05E9) and SIN-DOT (0x05C2) codepoint bytes together; ctx coverage fragments the deep switch-arm signal across call contexts; I2S diverts energy without helping this equality.
one_line: naive havoc assembles SHIN+SIN-DOT codepoint pair; ctx and I2S both fail

mechanism_summary: naive havoc converges on the specific 4-byte LE codepoint (8b/8d 14 01 00); ctx coverage inflates the switch edge map with sibling Tirhuta cases, dispersing energy across neighboring codepoints and blocking convergence on 0x1148B/0x1148D.
one_line: ctx inflates switch map with sibling codepoints; naive converges on 0x1148B/0x1148D
